([IATA](/wiki/IATA_airport_code "IATA airport code"): **LBV**, [ICAO](/wiki/ICAO_airport_code "ICAO airport code"): **FOOL, French: *Aéroport international Léon-Mba***) is an airport situated in [Libreville](/wiki/Libreville "Libreville"), [Gabon](/wiki/Gabon "Gabon"). Constructed in the 1950s, it is the main international airport in the country.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

**Léon-Mba International Airport** ([IATA](/wiki/IATA_airport_code "IATA airport code"): **LBV**, [ICAO](/wiki/ICAO_airport_code "ICAO airport code"): **FOOL, French: *Aéroport international Léon-Mba***) is an airport situated in [Libreville](/wiki/Libreville "Libreville"), [Gabon](/wiki/Gabon "Gabon"). Constructed in the 1950s, it is the main international airport in the country.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Airlines and destinations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=L%C3%A9on-Mba_International_Airport&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Airlines and destinations")]

### Passenger

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=L%C3%A9on-Mba_International_Airport&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Passenger")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d8/B777_d%27Air_France_%C3%A0_LBV.jpg/250px-B777_d%27Air_France_%C3%A0_LBV.jpg)](/wiki/File:B777_d%27Air_France_%C3%A0_LBV.jpg)

[Air France](/wiki/Air_France "Air France") [Boeing 777](/wiki/Boeing_777 "Boeing 777") arrived from [Charles de Gaulle Airport](/wiki/Charles_de_Gaulle_Airport "Charles de Gaulle Airport"), France.

## Accidents and incidents

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=L%C3%A9on-Mba_International_Airport&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Accidents and incidents")]

* On 2 April 1977, an [Aviogenex](/wiki/Aviogenex "Aviogenex") (Yugoslavia) [Tupolev Tu-134](/wiki/Tupolev_Tu-134 "Tupolev Tu-134") registered YU-AJS, crashed on landing, killing the eight crew on board.[[18]](#cite_note-18)
* On 8 June 2004, a [Gabon Express](/wiki/Gabon_Express "Gabon Express") twin-engine [Hawker Siddeley HS 748](/wiki/Hawker_Siddeley_HS_748 "Hawker Siddeley HS 748") operating [Gabon Express Flight 221](/wiki/Gabon_Express_Flight_221 "Gabon Express Flight 221") ditched into the [Gulf of Guinea](/wiki/Gulf_of_Guinea "Gulf of Guinea") after suffering an engine failure and hydraulic problems shortly after take-off, leading to the deaths of 19 out of the 30 onboard.[[19]](#cite_note-19)
* On 6 June 2011, [Antonov An-26](/wiki/Antonov_An-26 "Antonov An-26") TR-LII of [Solenta Aviation](/wiki/Solenta_Aviation "Solenta Aviation"), operating Flight 122A for [DHL Aviation](/wiki/DHL_Aviation "DHL Aviation") ditched in the sea near Libreville International Airport.[[20]](#cite_note-Wire-20)[[21]](#cite_note-AH43dbcc52-21) Four people on board were rescued and transported to a local hospital, but were not seriously injured.[[20]](#cite_note-Wire-20) The crew reported hydraulic problems and eyewitnesses stated that the aircraft's propellers were not turning at the time of the ditching.[[21]](#cite_note-AH43dbcc52-21)
